content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward methods like ICM and RND can be re-weighted by an agent's available working memory capacity to avoid the well-documented problem of unproductive infinite loops over low-complexity stochastic noise. This creates a natural "exploration stop rule" that aligns an agent's curiosity with its actual ability to encode and reason about new information.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
